field_inventory.description: Self-describing inventory of mutable state.json fields with expected refresh cadence. During metric verification (step 5.11), any field not refreshed within its cadence should be flagged and corrected. This inverts the verification pattern from 'enumerate what to check' to 'flag what hasn't been checked.' Per audit #80. Semantics: last_refreshed = cycle when entry was last checked (value confirmed unchanged or updated). This differs from per-field last_verified which means 'cycle when value was independently measured.' A field can be refreshed (checked and confirmed unchanged) without being re-verified (re-measured).

audit_dropped.cadence: after dropping a previously accepted audit recommendation

cycle_phase.notes: Tracks cycle, phase, phase_entered_at, and completed_at when phase is complete.

eva_input_issues.closed_this_cycle.cadence: every cycle (reset even when no closures)

last_eva_comment_check.cadence: every cycle (checked even when no comments found)

qc_requests_pending.cadence: after QC request creation or completion

review_agent.cadence: every cycle (updated when consuming review findings)

review_agent.chronic_category_responses.cadence: when chronic categories are detected (5+ in last 6 reviews)

review_events_verified_through_cycle.cadence: every cycle (after verifying review events on merged PRs)

schema_status.google_rich_results_types.cadence: after Google gallery changes or new implementations

schema_status.phpstan_max_assessment.cadence: after PHPStan config or fix changes

test_count.cadence: every merge that adds/removes PHP or TS tests (scope: PHP+TS only, excludes Rust tool tests)

total_testable_types.cadence: after schema class or enum additions

typescript_stats.cadence: every merge that adds/removes TS files

chronic_category_responses.description: Traceable artifacts for chronic category escalation (step 0.5.8). When a finding category appears in 5+ of the last 6 reviews, it must have a response entry here documenting root cause, chosen path, and verification. Per cycle 159/160 review recommendation.

entries.rationale: journal-quality findings in cycles 155-160 were positive/confirmatory (5/5 positive). Recurrence reflects consistent verification of a healthy practice, not a chronic problem. No structural fix needed — the review agent should continue checking journal quality but the category is expected to appear regularly.
entries.root_cause: Review agent consistently evaluates journal quality each cycle. Findings are overwhelmingly positive (confirming reflective, causally-linked entries). The category recurs because journal evaluation is a standing review criterion, not because of a persistent deficiency.

entries.rationale: process-adherence findings in cycles 195-200 consistently flagged missing per-step comments on cycle issues. The structural fix is the post-step tool (merged cycle 200, PR #846) plus the existing STARTUP_CHECKLIST mandate. Cycle 201 is the first cycle using post-step for every step. If the category persists after tool adoption, recalibrate.
entries.root_cause: Orchestrator posted batched summary comments instead of individual per-step comments. The post-step tool did not exist until cycle 200, and was not used in its merge cycle due to the adoption gap pattern (audit #160).

entries.rationale: receipt-integrity findings in cycles 197-203 consistently flagged nonexistent SHA hashes and stale metrics in hand-assembled worklog receipt tables. The structural fix has two layers: (1) cycle-receipts tool (merged cycle 200) generates receipts mechanically from git log, and (2) cycle-close enhancement (#884, dispatched cycle 204) will embed receipt tables in closing comments automatically. The root cause is manual assembly — the fix is automation.
entries.root_cause: Orchestrator hand-assembled receipt tables by copying SHAs from tool output, sometimes recording wrong or stale hashes. The cycle-receipts tool exists but was not consistently used to generate the worklog receipt table.

entries.rationale: Resolution condition recalibrated per audit #271 (cycle 283). Original condition (zero worklog-accuracy findings) was asymptotically unreachable: the review agent reliably finds timing-inherent drift every cycle (stale pre-dispatch state snapshots, post-worklog commits) because the worklog is structurally written before the review dispatch. Recalibrated condition: only factual worklog errors (wrong links, wrong counts, fabricated data) block resolution. Timing-inherent drift (pre-dispatch snapshots, structural exclusions) is accepted as inherent to the write-then-dispatch architecture. The structural fix (write-entry tool + phased architecture) addresses factual accuracy. Timing drift is a non-defect characteristic of the workflow.
entries.root_cause: write-entry defaults to 'Not provided.' for --copilot-metrics, --publish-gate when CLI flags are not passed, instead of reading from state.json. The orchestrator does not always pass these flags. The deeper root cause is that the orchestrator both does the work and writes its own report — no separation of concerns. Secondary root cause: timing-inherent drift where worklog is written before review dispatch, making the state snapshot structurally stale.

entries.rationale: Re-filed cycle 267 per review cycle 266 F1: previous cross-check only verified freshness-marker/value consistency, not that the value was derived from actual GitHub review events. Third structural fix: dispatch tool to query PR review events from GitHub API and verify review_events_verified_through_cycle against real data. Cycle 268 review identified verify-review-events tool has correctness gaps (accepts any review state, fail-open). Downgraded cycle 269. Cycle 270: PR #1300 merged hardened tool (APPROVED-only filter, self-review rejection, post-merge rejection, fail-closed). Tool re-run verified successfully against cycles 269-270 (marker advanced to 270). Unit tests cover all filtering paths. Code-PR hard case will be naturally tested at next schema implementation merge.
entries.root_cause: Three sub-causes: (1) cycle-complete mechanically bumping freshness markers (fixed cycle 243). (2) Orchestrator manually bumping markers without verification (caught by cross-check cycle 266). (3) Cross-check itself is superficial — passes on manually asserted values (identified cycle 266 review F1). (4) verify-review-events tool accepts any review state and fails open on missing PRs (fixed cycle 270, PR #1300).

entries.rationale: Dispatched #1176 to fix write-entry auto-derivation gating on args.cycle instead of resolved cycle, and to add regression tests for the default (omitted-cycle) invocation path. The pattern generator is insufficient test coverage for default invocation paths.
entries.root_cause: Tool code is merged with tests that only exercise the explicit-argument path (cycle: Some(N)), not the default production path (cycle: None, resolved from state.json). Bugs in the default invocation are invisible to CI.

entries.rationale: journal-quality findings in cycles 244-249 are critical (phantom receipt hashes, premature closure claims, disposition language inaccuracies). Unlike the cycle 161 recalibration (positive confirmatory findings), these are genuine defects. The root cause is manual journal generation — receipt hashes copied from memory, disposition language not validated against authoritative sources. The structural fix is improving write-entry to auto-derive journal receipt data from cycle-receipts output and validate disposition language against state.json. This is the same root cause as chronic worklog-accuracy. Partial fix merged (write-entry PR #1197 cycle 251). Remaining root cause: stale commitments generated before close-out steps complete. Category persists at 3 reviews since cycle 250.
entries.root_cause: Journal follow-through receipts and disposition claims are hand-written by the orchestrator rather than derived from cycle-receipts or state.json. This produces phantom hashes (F3 cycle 249) and premature closure claims (F4 cycle 249).

entries.rationale: Behavioral part adopted (gh pr review --approve since cycle 260). Tool hardened cycle 270 (PR #1300): APPROVED-only filter, self-review rejection, post-merge rejection, fail-closed on missing PRs. Tool re-run cycle 270 verified successfully against cycles 269-270 (marker advanced to 270). All filtering paths covered by unit tests.

phases.description: Ongoing iteration. Pipeline-check runs 10 phases: metric-snapshot, field-inventory, housekeeping-scan, cycle-status, state-invariants, derive-metrics, artifact-verify, doc-validation, step-comments, current-cycle-steps. Write-side tools: cycle-start, process-merge, process-review, record-dispatch, cycle-complete, write-entry, post-step. cycle-runner (Phase 2b, cycle 308) composes startup and close-out sequences. Stabilization program (ADR 0011) completed cycle 313 with 6 consecutive clean cycles (308-313). Post-stabilization: addressing close-out ordering defect (audit 297).

total_testable_types_note: 89 schema classes - 12 enums = 77 testable.
